sources.notes: Impartial game with tokens in columns; players choose tokens and spawn lower-column tokens according to the rule.
Official editorial reduces the game to parity of token counts in columns: column 2 for n=1, and all columns >=2 for n>=2.

annotation.summary: Determine the winner of a finite token-spawning game on columns.
annotation.constraints: Only the parity of occupied columns matters, so k token coordinates can be processed directly.
annotation.core_idea: The game always terminates because the vector of column counts decreases lexicographically. For n=1, a token in column c contributes 2^(c-2) future moves, so only tokens in column 2 affect the winner's parity. For n>=2, a position is losing exactly when every column i>=2 has even token count; if some highest odd column exists, the current player can choose a move that makes all relevant parities even for the opponent.
annotation.complexity: O(k) per test case using parity toggles.

solution_variants.name: parity-game
solution_variants.summary: Toggle the parity of relevant columns and decide the winner by whether any relevant parity is odd.

tags.evidence: The solution classifies losing and winning states of an impartial finite game.
The only relevant state information is the parity vector of token counts in columns >=2.
When a highest odd column exists, the winning move greedily fixes parities below it to hand over a losing state.
